title: Pressure Drop
setting: The Aegis-7 research rig, North Sea. High winds, salt spray, and a localized gravity anomaly.

characters.name: The Engineer
characters.description: A seasoned structural inspector who has seen everything—except this.

items.name: Thermal Cutter
items.description: A handheld plasma torch. It can cut through reinforced bulkhead steel with a single pull of the trigger.

items.name: Security Override Key
items.description: A heavy industrial key-fob with the Aegis-7 logo. It feels oily and vibrates slightly.

items.name: Crane Remote
items.description: A weather-beaten control unit with a single joy-stick and a large 'LOAD' button.

items.name: Black Box Recorder
items.description: A orange, salt-crusted sphere. It's emitting a low-frequency pulse that makes your teeth ache.

items.name: Distress Beacon
items.description: An emergency transmitter. Its battery is low, but it should have enough power for one last burst.

scenes.name: Rig Deck
scenes.prose: The Aegis‑7 rig is a skeletal giant of rusted steel, moaning under the lash of a North Sea gale. Salt spray stings your eyes as you climb from the helicopter onto the slick deck. Torrents of grey rain wash away the thick patches of industrial oil. To the north, the bulkhead door to the command center is jammed shut by a buckled frame, its metal glowing with an internal heat that defies the freezing wind. A neglected emergency locker hangs open nearby, revealing a thermal‑cutter resting among the rags.

lookDescriptions.rusted-steel: The very foundations of the rig are shivering. It’s not just the wind.
lookDescriptions.emergency-locker: Battered and salt‑stained. Inside, the thermal‑cutter is clean and ready.
lookDescriptions.jammed-bulkhead: The steel has been warped as if by a massive, invisible hand.

scenes.name: Command Center
scenes.prose: The air inside is static‑charged and smells of burnt wiring. This room was a high‑tech nerve center, now it's a graveyard of shattered glass and overturned consoles. Red emergency lights pulse in time with the deep, rhythmic thudding from the rig's legs. To the west, a heavy door leads into the roaring machine room. To the east, a pressure‑sealed airlock waits, its electronic display reading: PRESSURE MISMATCH. A central terminal stands in the middle of the chaos, its screen flickering blue and demanding a security‑override‑key.

lookDescriptions.shattered-consoles: Someone was trying to shut the rig down in a hurry.
lookDescriptions.airlock-door: Reinforced titanium. It won’t budge until the internal pressure is equalized.
lookDescriptions.central-terminal: The main interface for the rig’s systems. It’s waiting for authorization.

scenes.name: Machine Room
scenes.prose: The noise here is absolute—a roaring, grinding cacophony of steam and grease. Massive engines pulse like the internal organs of a beast, leaking hot fluid onto the vibrating floor. Steam vents hiss, casting long, shifting shadows against the bulkheads. Behind a vibrating ventilation grille, you catch the glint of a security‑override‑key, snagged on a metal fan blade. The heat is suffocating, and the exit back to the command center is the only escape from the din.

lookDescriptions.roaring-engines: They’re running at 110% capacity. They’re going to blow.
lookDescriptions.vent-grille: Rattling violently. The key behind it is just out of reach without the right angle.
lookDescriptions.steam-clouds: Hot and opaque. You can barely see your own hands.

scenes.name: The Airlock
scenes.prose: The airlock is a narrow, claustrophobic cylinder of cold grey metal. A pile of discarded, shredded wet‑suits lies in the corner, looking like the skin of a shed creature. A crane‑remote sits atop the pile, its yellow plastic casing cracked. A heavy floor hatch leads down into the dive‑bay, the ladder slick with grease. The air here is thick with the smell of old blood and ozone.

lookDescriptions.shredded-suits: They weren't cut—they were torn from the inside out.
lookDescriptions.floor-hatch: Leads deep into the rig's structural substructure.
lookDescriptions.remote-unit: Designed for heavy industrial use. It feels sturdy in your grip.

scenes.name: Dive Bay
scenes.prose: The dive‑bay is a cathedral of dark water and cold iron. In the center, a massive moonpool chugs and swirls, the dark ocean below visible and hungry. A hydraulic‑crane looms over the water, its cable dangling into the abyss. To the north, a reinforced door leads to the observation‑dome, its viewport dark. The depth gauges on the wall continue to spin, reading depths that shouldn't exist in the North Sea. Something is dragging the rig down.

lookDescriptions.moonpool: A shifting circle of black water. It tastes of the deep abyss.
lookDescriptions.hydraulic-crane: A massive piece of equipment. It looks like it just hauled something heavy up.

scenes.name: Observation Dome
scenes.prose: The dome is a panoramic glass eye peering into the crushing dark. Outside, the water is a wall of blackness, illuminated only by the faint, pulsing blue glow of something enormous moving in the distance. Clutched in the skeletal hand of a missing crewman, a distress‑beacon flickers with a failing red light. A transmitter‑dish stands in the center of the dome, aimed at the surface he can no longer reach. The glass of the dome is beginning to spiderweb with cracks under the impossible pressure.

lookDescriptions.panoramic-glass: The web of cracks is growing. You are at the bottom of the world.
lookDescriptions.missing-crewman: He died watching the thing outside. His expression is one of pure awe.
lookDescriptions.transmitter-dish: Your last hope. It’s wired directly to the rig’s main antenna.

puzzles.result: The thermal cutter carves through the steel in a shower of sparks. The bulkhead door groans open, revealing the dark command center.
The terminal chirps. The pressure monitors turn blue and the airlock to the east cycles open with a rhythmic hiss.
The crane whirrs to life, dragging a salt‑crusted research capsule from the depths. It deposits the sphere on the deck, and a black‑box‑recorder rolls out.
The screams in the recording match the frequency of the observation dome’s lock. A heavy clonk anchors the door—the dome is now accessible.
You activate the beacon. A blinding burst of light illuminates the abyss, revealing the creature's eye outside. The signal is sent.

winCondition.description: The pulse illuminates the water outside the dome—revealing a creature the size of the rig itself. The signal is sent. As the glass begins to crack from the pressure of a massive tentacle, you see the rescue lights in the distance. You've done your part. The end.
